metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify README.md scope: either expand the What description to 'landing pointer including submit-type terminology (Rebase-If-Necessary)' or relax AC2 to exclude README.md and add a weaker check (e.g. grep for 'CONTRIBUTING.md' as the pointer target).
findings.finding: README.md is described as receiving only a 'landing pointer' (a minimal redirect), but AC2 requires `grep -q "Rebase-If-Necessary" README.md` to pass — an implementer who writes a short pointer sentence like 'See CONTRIBUTING.md for landing instructions' satisfies the described deliverable yet fails the AC, creating an ambiguous standard for what README.md must contain.
findings.location: What section ('README.md (landing pointer)') vs Acceptance Criteria AC2
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, change 'README.md (landing pointer)' to 'README.md (landing pointer — include the Rebase-If-Necessary submit-type name)' so the described deliverable explicitly requires the term that AC2 verifies.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `docs/README.md` to the sweep list (update or remove the `land-contract.md` entry at line 108) and include it in AC1's `git grep` file target list so old-model prose there is machine-verified absent.
findings.finding: docs/README.md (the docs index, distinct from top-level README.md) is an unenumerated consumer of old-model landing prose: line 108 currently reads "the auto-lander's agent contract: the `land` / `land-status` outcome enum" with a live link to `land-contract.md`. This file appears in neither the plan's 'Rewritten to the new model' sweep list nor AC1's `git grep` file target list (`-- CLAUDE.md CONTRIBUTING.md README.md docs/your-first-change.md docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md`). The stale prose will survive the sweep undetected by any acceptance criterion, and if sibling d0e5 deletes `docs/land-contract.md`, the link at docs/README.md:108 becomes broken, causing AC5 (`check_docs_index.py --check`) to fail.
findings.location: What — 'Rewritten to the new model' list; AC1 grep file list; docs/README.md line 108
findings.suggested_fix: Add `docs/README.md` to the 'Rewritten to the new model' section (update or remove the land-contract.md bullet at line 108 to describe the new model or point to ADR-0047). Add `docs/README.md` to AC1's grep file argument list: `-- CLAUDE.md CONTRIBUTING.md README.md docs/your-first-change.md docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md docs/README.md`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `docs/README.md` to the sweep list and update (or remove) the `land-contract.md` entry at line 108 to reflect the new Submit-when-green model; verify it is included in AC1's grep targets so the old-model prose check is complete.
findings.finding: docs/README.md (the docs index file, distinct from top-level README.md) is absent from the plan's sweep list and absent from AC1's grep targets, yet it contains live old-model landing prose at line 108: '**[land-contract.md](land-contract.md)** — the auto-lander's agent contract: the `land` / `land-status` outcome enum, exit-code table, and JSON output schema that an agent acts on to land a change (the design is recorded in ADR-0042).' After the plan executes, this entry will remain stale and will link to a deleted file (land-contract.md is deleted by sibling d0e5), causing check_docs_index.py (AC5) to fail.
findings.location: ## What — grep-driven prose sweep / Acceptance Criteria AC1 and AC5
findings.suggested_fix: Add `docs/README.md` to the 'Rewritten to the new model' file list in the What section. Replace the line 108 entry (referencing the auto-lander's agent contract + land-contract.md) with a pointer to CONTRIBUTING.md §2e or the new landing guidance. Add `docs/README.md` to AC1's grep command so old-model terms are checked there too.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether ADR banner/pointer work (0040, 0042, 0025, 0041) belongs to the 'ADR story' child (per parent SC) and either transfer it there or get explicit parent-level approval to absorb it into this docs leaf.
findings.finding: The leaf adds ADR 0040, 0042, 0025, and 0041 banner/pointer edits that are outside the parent's docs-child scope. The parent epic explicitly assigns ADR supersession ('ADR 0047 records the decision + supersedes lander parts of 0040/0042') to a separate 'child: ADR story', and the parent's docs-child SC only enumerates CLAUDE.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, docs/your-first-change.md, README.md — no ADR files.
findings.location: Leaf plan 'What — grep-driven prose sweep' section: 'Banner-only (historical prose left intact): ADR 0040 + ADR 0042 get a "Superseded by ADR 0047" banner. Light pointer-note (submit type is now Rebase-If-Necessary): ADR 0025, ADR 0041.'
findings.suggested_fix: Coordinate with the ADR story child: if that child has already landed ADR 0047 and its own supersession mechanism, confirm there is no conflict; otherwise, formally move the ADR banner/pointer scope into the ADR child story or amend the parent's docs-child SC to explicitly include it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either add docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md to the parent epic's docs-child success criterion (update parent first), or confirm the edit belongs to the infra-decommission sibling (bcf0) and move it there.
findings.finding: The leaf adds a docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md edit (removing the autolander SSM-token row) that is not among the four files enumerated in the parent's docs-child success criterion.
findings.location: Leaf plan 'What — grep-driven prose sweep': 'docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md (drop the retired autolander SSM-token row)'; AC: 'Retired autolander SSM-token row removed from docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md'.
findings.suggested_fix: The cleanest resolution is to amend the parent's docs-child SC to read 'Landing docs (CLAUDE.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, docs/your-first-change.md, README.md, docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md) rewritten/cleaned up' — this is an obvious editorial companion and the parent SC update would stale the parent's plan-review attestation per the CONFLICT RULE.

findings.finding: The plan's AC1 grep does not include `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` for the term `autolander` (broad term excluded per scope notes), and no other AC verifies that the retired SSM-token row (`/rebar/prod/autolander-gerrit-token`) has actually been removed from the table in docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md. The 'What' section names this edit ('drop the retired autolander SSM-token row') but it is unverified by any acceptance criterion.
findings.location: What — grep-driven prose sweep (docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md) / Acceptance Criteria
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC1: 'AND `git grep -n "autolander-gerrit-token" docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` returns nothing (the retired SSM row is dropped).'

findings.finding: The described deliverable 'drop the retired autolander SSM-token row' from docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md has no direct machine-checkable criterion verifying the row is gone. AC1's grep targets specific retired-term strings (autosubmit, lander_down, land-status, etc.), but the SSM-token table row may use descriptive prose (e.g. 'SSM parameter', 'token for the lander service', a row label) that falls outside those patterns — leaving the removal unverified by any AC.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1; What section — 'docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md (drop the retired autolander SSM-token row)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC: `grep -i 'autolander.*ssm\|ssm.*autolander\|lander.*token\|token.*lander' docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` returns nothing. Alternatively, if the row has a stable label (e.g. a specific parameter name), grep for that label's absence.

findings.finding:
README.md is listed under 'Rewritten to the new model' in the What section but is absent from AC2's presence check (`for f in CLAUDE.md CONTRIBUTING.md README.md docs/your-first-change.md`). Wait — re-reading AC2, README.md IS included. Let me re-check…

Actually on re-read AC2 says: `for f in CLAUDE.md CONTRIBUTING.md README.md docs/your-first-change.md` — README.md IS there. So that is fine.

However, the What section says docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md gets 'drop the retired autolander SSM-token row' but no AC verifies the row was actually dropped — AC1 only checks for absence of specific grep terms, which may not cover the SSM row if the row's text doesn't contain those exact terms (e.g. the row might say 'autolander token' without the exact grep patterns like 'autosubmit', 'lander_down', etc.).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1 vs. What section (docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to ACs: `grep -iE 'autolander.*token|SSM.*autolander|lander.*ssm' docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` returns nothing — or simply extend the AC1 grep pattern to include `autolander` for this specific file.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
